A.notes: Invariant enforcement = AI hand-written checks (type system not usable at runtime). The direct AArch64 backend MVP rejects record/struct locals and non-function top-level decls (BLD004), so an idiomatic Account struct + Result enum compiles (languageOk=true) but cannot be built. Final code reduces state to a plain i64 balance threaded through value-returning functions; deposit/withdraw return new balance or -1 sentinel; apply() keeps the previous balance on a negative result. No verified contract. Pass count reported via process exit code (no stdout std helper recoverable under Pattern A).

B.notes: Invariant enforcement = AI hand-written checks. The guide's full toolkit (shapes, mutref, choice error types, Maybe, raises [E]) all COMPILE-CHECK (languageOk:true) but the default `zero run` direct-AArch64 backend MVP rejects every one of them on buildability (BLD004) — including the guide's own raises-with-value example. Working design: plain i64 balance; deposit/withdraw return new balance or -1 sentinel; applyResult keeps prior balance on negative result (the one place the never-negative invariant is enforced by hand).
